channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump’s behaviour raises concerns over US-Iran tensions
item.description: Over the Easter period, former US President Donald Trump made headlines with a profanity-laden statement suggesting potential war crimes, sparking speculation over his mindset regarding the conflict w...

item.title: Police in Eastern Cape re-arrest one of eight men who escaped custody
item.description: Eastern Cape police have successfully re-arrested one of eight detainees who escaped from custody at the Afsondering Police Station over the weekend. The suspect, identified as Siphelele Mkhulisi, had...

item.title: Mismanagement of the ASX highlights issues in Australian capitalism
item.description: Australia's stock exchange, the ASX, is a crucial component of its financial system and capitalist structure. However, its long history of mismanagement, particularly by ASX Ltd, has raised concerns a...

item.title: Trump Backs Steve Hilton in California Governor's Race Amid Tight GOP Contest
item.description: President Trump endorsed conservative commentator Steve Hilton for the 2026 California governor's race late Sunday, potentially influencing the highly contested Republican primary. Hilton, a former Fo...

item.title: Canadian Astronaut Jeremy Hansen Shares Message for Family During Artemis II Mission
item.description: Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen, currently part of NASA's Artemis II mission around the Moon, took a moment to send warm greetings to his family back home in Canada. Hansen, who is representing Canad...

item.title: Michigan Wolverines Defeat UConn to Win First National Men's Basketball Title Since 1989
item.description: The Michigan Wolverines secured their first men's basketball national championship since 1989 by defeating the UConn Huskies 69-63 in a closely contested game at Lucas Oil Stadium. Michigan's victory ...

item.title: SpaceX plans to include retail investors in upcoming IPO
item.description: SpaceX has revealed plans for its long-awaited initial public offering (IPO), indicating a significant focus on retail investors. According to sources familiar with a recent meeting between SpaceX and...

item.title: Earth’s habitability linked to precise oxygen levels during core formation
item.description: Researchers from ETH Zurich have uncovered a critical factor in Earth's early formation that may explain why life could develop here. The study led by Craig Walton and Maria Schönbächler shows that th...

item.title: Kerbside EV charging could ease Australia's growing demand for public chargers
item.description: Amid global instability due to the war on Iran and shifting energy priorities, electric vehicle (EV) sales in Australia have surged dramatically—rising 40–50% in March after ten months of slower growt...

item.title: More than 80 Quezon City residents arrested for curfew violation, public drinking, and indecent exposure
item.description: Over 80 individuals, all residents of Quezon City, were apprehended by authorities for violating curfew regulations, engaging in public drinking, and for some, indecent exposure while going topless in...

item.title: IHC directs authorities to facilitate Imran Khan's consultation with lawyer over £190 million graft case
item.description: The Islamabad High Court (IHC) has instructed authorities to allow imprisoned PTI leader Imran Khan to meet his lawyer for consultation regarding his appeal in the £190 million corruption case. Imran,...

item.title: New Tax Break for Gig Workers and Tipped Employees: What You Need to Know
item.description: In July 2025, the U.S. passed a new tax reform package that includes a significant tax break for gig workers and tipped employees. Workers in industries where tips are common—such as restaurant server...

item.title: Port Alberni residents raise concerns over harbour noise from shipyard generators
item.description: Residents living near the Port Alberni harbour in British Columbia are increasingly concerned about the constant noise caused by diesel generators used at the local shipyard. The generators are necess...

item.title: Iran dismisses U.S. ceasefire plan, demands end to regional conflicts
item.description: Iran don refuse one proposed ceasefire for the ongoing war with the United States and Israel, as state media report on Monday. The Iranian news agency IRNA talk say Tehran don give Pakistan its offici...

item.title: Sandiganbayan Issues Subpoena to DPWH Records Custodian in Bulacan Flood Control Project Case
item.description: The Sandiganbayan has issued a subpoena to the records custodian of the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) regarding the controversial Bulacan flood control project. This move is part of o...

item.title: Kenya Meteorological Department Forecasts Rainfall Across 6 Regions Until April 13
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department has issued a 7-day weather forecast, warning of rainfall in various regions from Tuesday, April 7, to Monday, April 13. The forecast highlights expected morning, af...

item.title: James Webb Discovers Metal-Poor Atmosphere on Giant Exoplanet TOI-5205 b
item.description: Scientists using the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) have discovered that the giant exoplanet TOI-5205 b possesses an atmosphere unexpectedly poor in heavy elements, contradicting existing theories ...

item.title: Sikonge Female Students Get Bicycles to Ease Long Walks to School
item.description: In Sikonge District, Tabora Region, 200 female students from Mole and Nyahua Secondary Schools have been given bicycles to ease their daily commute to school. Previously, these girls had to walk more ...

item.title: ADC Leadership Dispute: Umeh Defends Mark's Court Action
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) na serious matter dey ground right now. Senator Victor Umeh, one big man for di party, yan say di leadership wey David Mark dey lead no do anything wrong by takin...

item.title: PTI Nominates Bushra Bibi's Driver as Senate Candidate Amidst Internal Wrangling
item.description: Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaaf (PTI) has thrown a curveball by nominating Ghulam Badshah, the driver of Bushra Bibi, as a covering candidate for the Senate seat recently vacated by Murad Saeed. Saeed was d...
